revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image for a game-development pixel-art asset.

Profile: snes16_platformer_v1
Preset: world_coastline_16
Asset kind: terrain-tile
Asset type: tile
Exact canvas size: 64x64 pixels.
Grid: 4 rows x 4 columns.
Frame size: 16x16 pixels.
Frames: 16.

Create a top-down transition tileset of a calm village pond meeting soft grassy banks in a bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette: clear blue-green water, deep matcha-green banks, soft warm sunlight, top-left light source. Pixel art must be crisp low-resolution 16-bit style with readable pixel clusters, consistent palette and scale, no antialiasing blur. Every tile cell must be completely filled edge to edge, seamless and tileable, and every frame must stay inside its own 16x16 cell. Do not draw visible grid lines, tile IDs, labels, borders, text, watermark, UI, logos, or characters.

STRICT 4x4 TILESET LAYOUT:
Row 1: four full CENTER water variations, pure rippling clear blue-green water only.
Row 2: edge transition tiles with grassy bank softly fading in on the North, South, West, and East side, in that order. The shore must be smooth and gentle: clear water -> pale shallows -> wet mossy margin -> deep matcha-green grass. No hard outline, no harsh rock wall, no sharp line at the waterline.
Row 3: outer corner transition tiles NW, NE, SW, SE, in that order, with soft rounded shorelines and matching gradual gradients.
Row 4: four accent center variations, still mostly calm water, with subtle sky reflection, fine soft ripples, tiny floating light flecks, a few small smooth mossy pebbles, and gentle shoreline-compatible accents.

Preset rules: world-map coastline tiles with curved land-water boundaries; include coast edges, corners, beaches/shallows, small wave highlights, and broken shoreline variants; avoid straight vertical or horizontal strips of water next to land. Use broad geographic material language, not close-up village ground detail. Shoreline gradients must align so neighboring tiles blend smoothly and naturally; avoid repeated square stamps, checkerboard reading, hard rectangular bands, harsh banding, gaps between cells, and visible seams.

World-map material rules: water should read as calm pond water with fine soft ripples and faint sky reflection; grassland banks should have broad color drift, regional mossy patches, and low-frequency texture. Only a few small smooth mossy pebbles scattered gently, never a hard rock border. Keep the style bright, cheerful, and cohesive.

Output requirements: one 64x64 PNG image, opaque if appropriate. Use solid #FF00FF magenta only for truly empty unused space, but there should be no empty unused space because every tile cell is filled edge to edge. If any empty margin remains, fill it with #FF00FF.

Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness; no samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, bird or companion creature, letter emblems, modern text, watermark, UI.
